prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合历年位次连续两年大幅后移趋势、2026年计划小幅扩招及本省物理类考生微增，在20 25年基准17036名基础上预计竞争继续加剧但幅度收窄；预测最低位次约17509名，区间17009–18009名。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the lowest ranking for science (physics category) was approximately 12943 → 11899 → 11244 → 14289 → 17036. Rankings moved forward in 2022–2023, but significantly shifted backward from 2024 onward, with a cumulative backward shift of about 5792 places in the last two years. The 2025 baseline is approximately 17036. Provincial science enrollment plan: 100 in 2021, 102 in 2022, 98 in 2023, 98 in 2024, 104 in 2025; 2026 plan is 108, an increase of 4 (about 3.8%) compared to 2025, slightly intensifying competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 and Double First-Class university; ranked approximately 67th in the 2026 Soft Science main ranking, fluctuating between 54–67 in recent years, overall stable. Reputation evaluation in 2023 was neutral/mixed, with no significant reputation events in 2026. Douyin popularity ranked 68th overall, rated as average, with no obvious enrollment gaps; overall public opinion stable. Safety Science and Engineering A+ (top nationally), Civil Engineering A-, Surveying and Mapping Science and Technology A-, highly consistent with the university's mining and safety characteristics, providing stable attraction for related majors.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Popular majors such as Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, and Electronic Information continue to have high social heat, closely related to the university's enrollment majors. The number of physics category candidates in the province in 2026 is about 267,844, a slight increase of about 0.53% compared to 2025, slightly intensifying competition. The university is located in Xuzhou, Jiangsu, in East China, offering some regional attraction to Jiangxi candidates.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the trend of significant backward shifts in historical rankings for two consecutive years, a slight expansion of the 2026 plan, and a slight increase in provincial physics category candidates, competition is expected to continue intensifying but at a reduced rate based on the 2025 baseline of 17036. Predicted lowest ranking is approximately 17509, with a range of 17009–18009.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: For rankings within the predicted range of 17009–18009, it is recommended to primarily apply for Tier 2 majors, with Tier 1 as a reach and Tier 3 as a safety. Candidates with rankings significantly better than the lower limit (e.g., within 17000) can focus on reaching for popular Tier 1 majors like Electrical Engineering and Automation and Computer Science. Candidates near the upper limit (around 18000) should prioritize Tier 2 Safety Science and Engineering (A+ discipline) as the core, supplemented by Tier 3 majors for safety.
